The '''hodiernal tense''' in Aelaki refers to events happening today, particularly later today. It is formed with the prefix '''go-''', derived from the word '''go''' meaning "day."

== Formation ==

The hodiernal is marked by the prefix '''go-''' attached to the verb stem, combined with a set of evidential, intentional, and modal suffixes.

== Evidential and modal suffixes ==

{| class="wikitable"
! Category !! Suffix !! Meaning
|-
| Auditory || '''-ngrom''' || The speaker has heard or been told the event is happening today
|-
| Witnessed || '''-nshë''' || The speaker has seen or witnessed the event today
|-
| Inferential || '''-nshëm''' || The speaker infers the event is happening today
|-
| Intentional || '''-ng''' || The speaker intends to perform the action today
|-
| Optative || '''-nkya''' || The speaker wishes or hopes the event will happen today
|-
| Deliberative || '''-nkyam''' || The speaker is considering whether the event should happen today
|}

== Usage ==

The hodiernal is the most fluid tense in the Aelaki system. Because "today" can encompass both past and future events within the same day, speakers often rely on explicit adverbials to clarify whether they mean earlier today or later today.

== Diachronic notes ==

The prefix '''go-''' derives from the noun '''go''' "day," grammaticalized into a tense marker that anchors events to the current day.

== See also ==

* [[Hesternal]] -- "yesterday"
* [[Crastinal]] -- "tomorrow"
* [[Present]]
* [[Past]]
* [[Future]]
* [[Mythic]]

[[Category:grammatical tenses]]
[[Category:git synced pages]]
